calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Compelling Compliance With Investigative Discovery
Off Calendar Feb-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Susan Saylor for petitioner and moving party

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
The petition for an order compelling investigative discovery pursuant to Government Code Section 12963.5(c) is: granted.
The objections, other than based on the right to privacy are overruled and stricken. The Court has considered the privacy objection and finds that the state interest outweighs any intrusion on the privacy of third party applicants or guests that might result from disclosure of said records. The Court makes no rulings as to the propriety of responding to the interrogatories pursuant to CCP Section 2030.230. ORDER SIGNED AND CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE EXECUTED BY THE CLERK. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Petition For Order Compelling Compliance With Investigative Discovery
Off Calendar Jan-12-2006 Continued To Feb-10-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Hearing on the petition for an order compelling investigative discovery pursuant to Government Code Section 12963.5(c) is continued to February 10, 2006 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 610. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
